briefings.headline: Regulators Publish Updated List of Distressed and Underserved Rural Middle-Income Areas
briefings.body: Federal agencies have released an updated list identifying distressed or underserved nonmetropolitan middle-income geographies. These designations are used under Community Reinvestment Act guidelines to determine where banks receive credit for certain lending and investment activities. Communities that appear on the list could attract increased financial institution activity, as banks seek qualifying investments in these areas.

sources.title: Agencies release list of distressed or underserved nonmetropolitan middle-income geographies
The Sewer Knows What You Ate

briefings.why_it_matters: Residents and businesses in designated rural communities could see greater access to bank lending, investment, and financial services as institutions direct resources toward these areas to meet regulatory requirements.

briefings.headline: Federal Agencies Issue Joint Guidance on Protecting Sensitive Data During Bank Examinations
briefings.body: Federal banking agencies have issued a joint statement addressing how highly sensitive information should be handled during bank examinations. The guidance aims to establish consistent standards for managing confidential data collected in the examination process. Banks subject to federal oversight could face updated expectations around data handling protocols as agencies work to standardize practices across institutions.

sources.title: Agencies issue joint statement on handling of highly sensitive information during bank examinations
Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former chief lending officer of Heritage State Bank

briefings.why_it_matters: Clearer federal standards for handling sensitive examination data could affect how banks manage confidential customer and institutional information during regulatory reviews, with potential implications for data security and compliance obligations.

briefings.headline: Trump Administration Set to Announce Saudi Nuclear Fuel Deal Amid Weapons Concerns
briefings.body: The Trump administration is expected to announce a deal that could allow Saudi Arabia to enrich nuclear fuel, raising alarms among U.S. lawmakers and Israeli officials who fear the kingdom could use a civilian nuclear program as a pathway to developing nuclear weapons. Critics warn the agreement could destabilize an already volatile region by enabling weapons-grade material production.

sources.title: Trump Administration to Announce Deal That Could Let Saudis Enrich Nuclear Fuel

briefings.why_it_matters: A Saudi nuclear enrichment capability could shift the balance of power across the Middle East, potentially triggering a regional arms race that raises security risks for civilians and allies worldwide.

briefings.headline: Scientists Discover First-Ever Contagious Cancer Spreading Among Fish in a Lake
briefings.body: Researchers have identified a transmissible tumor spreading among fish, marking the first known case of contagious cancer in the species. Until now, transmissible tumors had only been documented in a handful of other animals. The discovery raises new questions about how such cancers spread in aquatic environments and whether similar mechanisms could exist more broadly across the animal kingdom, including implications for understanding cancer transmission generally.

sources.title: Deep in This Lake, a Contagious Cancer Is Spreading

briefings.why_it_matters: Expanding the known range of species capable of hosting contagious cancers could prompt scientists to reassess assumptions about cancer transmission, with long-term implications for both wildlife health monitoring and human medical research.

briefings.headline: Political oversight of federal grants could narrow scope of education research, experts warn
briefings.body: A proposed 100-page overhaul of federal grant rules would add a review layer by political appointees over research funding decisions. Education research organizations warn the change could constrain the kind of broad, multi-disciplinary work that produced the science of reading movement, which drew on cognitive science, neuroscience, psychology, linguistics, and classroom studies developed over decades of federally supported research.

sources.title: Education researchers warn political review of federal grants could reshape what gets studied

briefings.why_it_matters: If politically appointed reviewers gain influence over which studies get funded, the evidence base schools and policymakers rely on to improve student outcomes could narrow or shift away from independent scientific inquiry.

briefings.headline: TreeSize shifts perpetual-license holders to subscription model for continued support
briefings.body: TreeSize, a disk-space analysis tool, is requiring perpetual-license holders to purchase a subscription to maintain access to support and updates, citing current economic conditions as the driver. Users who previously paid a one-time fee could lose access to future patches and assistance unless they agree to recurring payments. The change reflects a broader industry shift away from traditional software licensing.

sources.title: TreeSize won't renew perpetual-license support unless users subscribe

briefings.why_it_matters: Customers who bought perpetual licenses expecting lifetime ownership could face unexpected ongoing costs or lose software support entirely if they decline the new subscription terms.

quick_hits.text: The EU has proposed changes to its Emissions Trading System as green policy backlash grows over costly industrial overhauls required to meet climate targets.

source.title: E.U. Proposes Changes to Emissions Trading System

quick_hits.text: SpaceX aborted Starship's 13th test flight after all 33 booster engines began igniting but were shut down before liftoff.

source.title: Will SpaceX’s 13th Test Flight of Starship Be Lucky?

quick_hits.text: More than one-eighth of apps used by U.S. troops contain embedded code traced to Chinese and Russian developers, a new analysis found.

source.title: Apps targeted at US troops contain Chinese and Russian code

quick_hits.text: Elementary students are routinely bypassing school Chromebook filters to access games and dangerous content, outsmarting district safeguards installed after pandemic-era remote learning expanded device use.

source.title: Schools try to block kids from accessing dangerous content and games online. Little kids are outsmarting them

quick_hits.text: Steam Deck sales plummeted 80 percent from 2025 levels after Valve raised the handheld gaming console's price.

source.title: Sales of Valve's Steam Deck crater after recent price hike

voice_blocks.title: This Day
voice_blocks.text: 2019: Chandrayaan-2, the second lunar exploration mission developed by Indian Space Research Organisation after Chandrayaan-1 is launched from Satish Dhawan Space Centre in a GSLV Mark III M1. It consists of a lunar orbiter, and also included the Vikram lander, and the Pragyan lunar rover.

closer.text: "The press was protected so that it could bare the secrets of government and inform the people."
closer.attribution: Justice Hugo Black, New York Times Co. v. United States (1971)

source.title: Justice Hugo Black, New York Times Co. v. United States (1971)
